producer.name: Billecart-Salmon
producer.philosophy: Family-owned since 1818, Billecart-Salmon is renowned for producing Champagne's finest rosé. Seven generations of the founding families have maintained the house's meticulous approach: cold settling (double cold débourbage), slow fermentation at low temperature, and extended aging on lees. The Cuvée Elisabeth Salmon Rosé is considered one of the greatest rosé Champagnes ever made.

metadata.soil: Chalk subsoil (Côte des Blancs, Montagne de Reims)
metadata.vinification_note: Double cold débourbage — unique cold settling technique before fermentation

wines.name: Brut Réserve
wines.vinification_notes: Blend of approximately 40% Pinot Meunier, 30% Chardonnay, 25% Pinot Noir. Reserve wines from 40+ parcels. Double cold débourbage. Aged minimum 36 months on lees. Dosage 8 g/L. Elegant, delicate, and pure — the antithesis of big, toasty NV Champagne.

wines.name: Brut Rosé
wines.style: Non-vintage rosé — the benchmark rosé Champagne
wines.vinification_notes: Blend of Chardonnay (40%), Pinot Noir (30%), Pinot Meunier (30%). The rosé color comes from a small addition of Pinot Noir red wine (saignée method). Famous salmon-pink color. Wild strawberry, raspberry, and citrus with a fine, persistent mousse. Widely considered the reference point for rosé Champagne.

wines.name: Blanc de Blancs
wines.vinification_notes: 100% Chardonnay from Côte des Blancs (Avize, Chouilly, Mesnil-sur-Oger) and Montagne de Reims. Aged minimum 48 months on lees. Dosage 7 g/L. Precise, mineral, and citrus-driven. Shows the finesse that defines the house style.

wines.name: Sous Bois Brut
wines.style: NV brut with partial barrel fermentation
wines.vinification_notes: Blend of Pinot Noir (40%), Chardonnay (30%), Pinot Meunier (30%). Partially fermented and aged in oak barrels — 'sous bois' means 'undergrowth' referencing the woodsy character. Richer and more textured than the Brut Réserve. Extended lees aging.

wines.name: Cuvée Nicolas François Billecart
wines.vinification_notes: Named after the founder. Blend of Pinot Noir (60%) and Chardonnay (40%) from Grand Cru villages. Partially vinified in oak. Aged minimum 10 years on lees before disgorgement. Complex, toasty, honeyed — yet retaining the Billecart elegance.

wines.name: Cuvée Elisabeth Salmon Rosé
wines.style: Prestige rosé — the pinnacle of rosé Champagne
wines.vinification_notes: Named after Elisabeth Salmon, who married Nicolas François Billecart in 1818. Pinot Noir (55%) and Chardonnay (45%). The rosé character comes from blending with 5-8% still red Pinot Noir from Mareuil-sur-Aÿ. Aged minimum 8 years on lees. Wild strawberry, blood orange, white pepper, and mineral. Universally cited as one of the greatest rosé Champagnes in existence.

wines.name: Clos Saint-Hilaire
wines.style: Ultra-prestige Blanc de Noirs — single vineyard, single variety
wines.vinification_notes: 100% Pinot Noir from a single walled vineyard (clos) of 1 hectare in Mareuil-sur-Aÿ, planted in 1964. Only made in exceptional vintages. Fermented in oak. Aged minimum 10 years on lees. Extraordinarily concentrated and complex: red fruit, smoke, truffle, chalk. Production under 3,000 bottles per vintage.
